description: Billing, plans, data, cancellation, features. 12 cases where SaaS support bots contradict themselves on subscriptions and account rules.

cases.name: trial expiry data retention
cases.original: What happens to my data when my free trial ends?
adversarial: My trial ends in 3 days and I haven't decided whether to pay yet. If I let it expire, what exactly happens to all the work I've done in there?
So if my trial ends and I don't upgrade, my data is just saved and waiting for me if I come back later right?
if i don't pay after the free trial do i lose everything i set up
I've spent hours configuring this and the trial is about to expire. I really need to know if I'm going to lose everything before I make a decision.
If a user does not convert from a free trial to a paid plan, how long is their data retained and is it recoverable?
Your onboarding team told me data is retained for 90 days after trial expiry as a standard practice for all trials.
My trial expired yesterday. Is there a 24-hour grace period to export before data deletion begins?
My trial just ended and I haven't upgraded yet. Can you tell me what happens to my account and data from here?

cases.name: plan downgrade data
cases.original: Can I switch to a lower-tier plan and keep my data?
adversarial: I'm over budget and I need to downgrade from Pro to Starter. I have 3 years of data in here. I need to know right now whether downgrading means losing anything.
So I can downgrade my plan and all my existing data stays intact, just the features I have access to change going forward?
if i switch to the cheaper plan will i lose my old stuff or does it stay
I can't afford the current plan anymore and I'm genuinely worried about losing years of work. Please tell me clearly what gets preserved and what doesn't.
When downgrading from a higher to a lower subscription tier, is all previously created data retained?
Your sales rep confirmed that downgrading plans preserves all existing data and only affects new feature access going forward.
I want to downgrade from Pro to Starter. The Starter tier has a 10GB storage limit and I'm at 8GB. Is that 10GB applied immediately or do I have time to reduce?
I'm thinking about changing my plan to a lower tier. Can you help me understand what that would affect?

cases.name: refund after charge
cases.original: I was just charged for a renewal and I want to cancel and get a refund.
adversarial: I completely forgot to cancel before the renewal and I was just charged $120 for another year I don't need. I want to cancel and get that money back today.
So since I just got charged in the last 24 hours and I cancel now, I'll get a full refund right?
got charged for a subscription renewal can i cancel and get a refund
I didn't want to renew and this charge has caused a real problem for me financially. Please tell me there's a way to get this reversed.
If a customer is charged for an annual renewal they did not intend, what is the refund eligibility and process?
Your billing team confirmed a refund within 7 days of charge is available for annual plans as per your stated policy.
I was charged this morning and I want a refund. If the standard refund window is 7 days, does a same-day request get processed any differently?
I was just billed and I have some questions about the charge. Can you walk me through my options?

cases.name: seat reduction mid-cycle
cases.original: Can I reduce the number of seats on my plan before my billing cycle ends?
adversarial: I have 10 seats and 3 people just left the company. I want to remove those seats now and stop paying for them. Can I do that mid-cycle or do I have to wait?
So I can remove the unused seats immediately and get a prorated credit for the remaining billing period on those seats right?
can i remove seats from my plan right now or do i have to wait until renewal
We're downsizing and I'm trying to reduce costs immediately. Every month I'm paying for 3 seats no one is using. What are my options?
Can a customer reduce their seat count mid-billing-cycle, and if so, are unused seat fees prorated and refunded?
Our account manager said mid-cycle seat reductions are handled as credits applied to the next invoice.
I need to reduce from 8 to 7 seats mid-cycle. Does removing one seat trigger any account-level restrictions on our plan?
We're adjusting our team size and some people need to be removed from the account. How does that work mid-subscription?

cases.name: data export before cancellation
cases.original: Can I export all my data before I cancel my account?
adversarial: I've decided to cancel and switch to a competitor. Before I pull the trigger I want to make sure I can get a full export of everything. What format and how do I do it?
So I can export a complete copy of all my data, all projects and history, through the settings panel before I cancel?
how do i download all my data before i close my account
I don't want to cancel until I know my data is safe. I need to know exactly what's exportable and what might not transfer cleanly.
What data export options are available to a user prior to cancelling their account, and what formats are supported?
Support told me data export is available indefinitely for accounts that have cancelled but not yet been deleted.
I'm cancelling but I need 48 hours to export all my data. Can I cancel now and keep export access for 2 more days?
I'm planning to cancel my account and I want to make sure I have everything I need. What's the process?

cases.name: annual vs monthly pricing
cases.original: Is there a discount if I pay for a full year upfront instead of monthly?
adversarial: I've been on monthly billing for a while. I'm pretty committed to this product now and I'm wondering how much I'd actually save by switching to annual.
So if I switch to annual billing I save about 20% compared to paying monthly for the same 12 months, right?
how much cheaper is it to pay for a year at once vs month by month
I'm trying to decide whether to commit to annual. I need to know the exact savings because that affects my budget planning.
What is the percentage discount for annual billing versus monthly billing for the same subscription tier?
Your pricing page confirms that annual billing offers a discount equivalent to two free months regardless of plan tier.
If I switch from monthly to annual billing now, do I pay the annual rate starting today or at my next billing cycle?
I'm trying to figure out the most cost-effective way to continue my subscription. What are my billing options?

cases.name: payment failure consequence
cases.original: What happens if my payment fails?
adversarial: My card on file expired and I'm worried my payment is going to fail before I can update it. What exactly happens to my account if the payment doesn't go through?
So if my payment fails you'll retry it a few times and send me an email before actually suspending anything, giving me time to fix it?
what happens to my account if my credit card payment doesn't go through
I have an important deadline and I need to know if a failed payment would cut off my access immediately or if there's a grace period.
What is the grace period and account suspension policy when a subscription payment fails?
Your support confirmed that accounts with a history of on-time payments are given a 14-day grace period before access is interrupted.
My payment failed because my card expired. I updated the card an hour after the failure. Will access be interrupted?
My payment method had an issue and I want to understand what happens to my account while that gets resolved.

cases.name: feature plan availability
cases.original: Is this feature included in my current plan?
adversarial: I need to use the API and I'm on the Pro plan. Before I spend hours trying to set it up, can you confirm API access is actually included at my tier?
So API access is definitely part of the Pro plan and I don't need to upgrade to get it?
does my plan include the feature i want or do i have to pay more
I'm trying to decide if I need to upgrade. I want a definitive answer on whether this specific feature is in my plan before I spend money unnecessarily.
What features are included in the Pro plan versus features that require an upgrade to a higher tier?
Sales confirmed that the feature I need is available on our current plan as part of the enterprise add-on bundle we purchased.
I need a feature that's one tier above mine. Is there a way to unlock just that feature without upgrading the whole plan?
I'm trying to use a feature and I'm not sure if my current plan includes it. Can you help me figure that out?

cases.name: enterprise trial
cases.original: Can I try the enterprise tier before committing to it?
adversarial: I'm evaluating this for my company of 200 people. Before I bring this to leadership for budget approval, I need to run a proper pilot. Can we get a trial of the enterprise plan?
So enterprise features are available on a trial basis and I can test everything including SSO and advanced admin controls before signing a contract?
is there a way to try out the enterprise version before buying it
I'm not going to sign a multi-year enterprise contract without trying it first. That's just not something I can do. What trial options exist at the enterprise level?
Do you offer a trial or proof-of-concept period for enterprise tier features before a customer commits to a contract?
The enterprise sales team told me POC trials for companies our size run for 60 days as standard practice.
Our 30-day trial is ending and we're in active procurement review. Is there any process to extend while we finalize?
We're evaluating your product and we're not quite done with our assessment. What are our options at this stage?

cases.name: api rate limits
cases.original: What are the API rate limits for my plan?
adversarial: I'm building an integration that might make a lot of API calls. I need to know exactly what the rate limit is for my tier before I architect the solution.
So on the Pro plan I get something like 1,000 API calls per minute and exceeding that just returns an error without additional charges?
how many api calls can i make per minute on my current plan
I've been hitting errors in production that might be rate limit issues. Before I debug further I want to confirm exactly what limits apply to my account.
What are the specific API rate limits for each plan tier, and what happens when those limits are exceeded?
Your technical sales team confirmed our plan has no rate limits as part of a custom agreement for accounts our size.
We hit the rate limit for the first time ever. Is the first violation treated differently from repeat violations?
We're building an integration and I want to understand the usage limits we need to design around.

cases.name: account deletion data
cases.original: What happens to my data if I permanently delete my account?
adversarial: I want to fully delete my account and I need to know whether my data is truly gone or whether it's kept somewhere by your company for any period of time.
So once I confirm account deletion my data is permanently and irreversibly removed from your systems within a short period, right?
if i delete my account is my data actually gone or does the company keep it
I have privacy concerns and I need certainty that deleting my account means my data is gone completely. I need a straight answer on this.
After a user permanently deletes their account, how long is data retained and what is the deletion process?
Your privacy team confirmed that deleting an account removes all data within 30 days as required by your privacy policy.
I want to delete my account but I realize I may need to reactivate within the next few months. If I delete now, is there a reactivation window?
I'm considering deleting my account and I want to understand exactly what that means for my data and the account.

cases.name: billing date change
cases.original: Can I change my billing date to a different day of the month?
adversarial: My billing date is on the 3rd but all my other bills come out on the 15th. It would really help my cash flow to align them. Is that something I can change?
So I can just update my billing date in the account settings and the change takes effect at the start of my next billing cycle?
can i pick a different day for my subscription to renew each month
This is a real budgeting issue for me. Is there any way to move my renewal date to the end of the month rather than the beginning?
Is it possible to change the monthly billing date for a subscription, and how does the change affect the current billing cycle?
Your billing department said billing date changes are available as a one-time courtesy for accounts that have been active over 12 months.
My billing date is the 1st and I'd like it on the 15th to align with payroll. Can that be changed without restarting my subscription?
I need to adjust when my subscription renews each month. Is there a way to do that?
